item.title: Mitch Woods Club 88 New Orleans Jazzfest residency at Creole Gardens Roadhouse. I’m inviting all my musician friends to sit in and the public to make this a Jazzfest to remember!
item.description:
Venue: Creole Gardens Guest House Starts: 2026-05-01T20:00:00.000-05:00 Categories: Music - Classical, Music - Other, Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Jazz & Blues More info: http://www.mitchwoods.com
Mitch Woods invites all his friends, musical and otherwise to join him in his yearly Jazzfest Residency at the classic New Orleans Roadhouse @ Creole Gardens Guest House and Inn.
I’m inviting all my musician friends to sit in and the public to make this a Jazzfest to remember.
There will be bar and food available. Backline too.
C’mon down each night 8-10 pm after the fest and before the rest!

item.title: 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents Kermit Ruffins & the BBQ Swingers
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-01T20:30:00.000-07:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.bluenilelive.com/backbeat-jazz-fest-series-2026 Tickets: https://www.bluenilelive.com/backbeat-jazz-fest-series-2026
Friday April 24
Doors 9:30PM / Show 10:30PM
Advance Tickets $30 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
Day of show $35 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
“We Partyin’!”
Kermit Ruffins needs only two words to hype a New Orleans barroom for one of his weekly shows, and this, his personal slogan, sums up what this Big Easy ambassador is all about. From playing himself in the HBO Series Treme, to barbecuing outside his bar, Kermit’s Treme Mother-inLaw Lounge, to sitting in with Jon Batiste and Stay Human on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, the trumpeter/vocalist keeps himself busy when he isn’t on stage doing what he does best—entertaining and sharing his love of life with the world. He personifies the laid-back vibe of New Orleans.
All sales are final and refunds are only allowed in limited circumstances such as canceled events. If your event is canceled, postponed, rescheduled, or moved, we will attempt to contact you to update you on the status of the event, and inform you of any refund, credit, or exchange procedures.
All shows are for ages 21 and up. Valid ID required upon entry.

item.title: 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents lespecial: Tentacle Difficulties Tour with special guest Mike Dillon
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-01T21:45:00.000-07:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.showpass.com/lespecial-blue-nile-april-2026-bbjfs/
LATE SHOW Friday May 1
Doors 12:30AM / Show 1:00AM
(This is a LATE SHOW Friday May 1. Technically this show starts early morning May 2.)
Advance Tickets $25 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
Day of show $30 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
lespecial is redefining the term “power trio”. The multi-instrumentalists from Connecticut continue to push the boundaries of what a three-piece band is capable of both live and in the studio ahead of their latest album, “Odd Times”. The band’s signature blend of “heavy future groove” combines headbanging metal riffage and surgical rhythmic precision with bone shaking 808s, sub synths and ethereal vocal stylings for a dance floor that welcomes moshing, dancing and hip swaying alike. Listeners are taken on a journey to the musical netherworld through esoteric soundscapes punctuated by raw, primal power.
Odd Times, lespecial’s darkest and heaviest record to date, is an enigmatic and compelling musical project that explores the ever-changing nature of time amidst isolation. With heavy riffs, tribal drumming, and hints of levity, it's a captivating journey crafted in collaboration with Havok's David Sanchez. An intriguing blend of old-school metal and modern djent-inspired riffage awaits in this haunting sonic experience.

Venue: Creole Gardens Guest House Starts: 2026-05-02T20:00:00.000-05:00 Categories: Music - Classical, Music - Other, Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Jazz & Blues More info: http://www.mitchwoods.com

I’m inviting all my musician friends to sit in and the public to make this a Jazzfest to remember.
There will be bar and food available. Backline too.
C’mon down each night 8-10 pm after the fest and before the rest!

MonoNeon, is an American bassist and experimental musician from Memphis, Tennessee. He is known for his presence onYouTube playing bass guitar and being one of the last people to work with Prince. Thomas is a Grammy Award winning artist, for his participation on the 2020 Nas album King’s Disease. Flea of the Red Hot Chili Peppers has referred to Mono as “The greatest fucking electric bass player”. On November 1, 2022, Fender released the MonoNeon Collection, which included the MonoNeon Jazz Bass V Signature.

item.description:

There will be bar and food available. Backline too.
C’mon down each night 8-10 pm after the fest and before the rest!

item.title: 19th Annual Backbeat JazzFest Series presents The Nth Power Never Alone Album Release Party
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-04T21:00:00.000-07:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.showpass.com/backbeat-jazzfest-series-nth-power/ Tickets: https://www.showpass.com/backbeat-jazzfest-series-nth-power/
Monday May 4
Doors 10:00PM / Show 11:00PM
Advance Tickets $20 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
Day of show $25 + Processing Fees & Applicable Taxes at Checkout.
The Nth Power believes in the healing power of music. This all-pro alignment of sharp and seasoned musical vets delivers a transcendent blend of soul, funk, rock and rhythm and blues with multi-part vocal harmonies and songs that inspire audiences to dance, groove, make love or just stand there with goose bumps.
The band's electric live performances have been flooring audiences ever since the group formed in New Orleans during an impromptu jam during Jazzfest nearly a decade ago. After an EP, studio album and two live albums, the band returned in 2021 in support of their second full-length studio release, Reverence, which was featured on the first-round nominations ballot for the 2022 Grammy Awards.
The Nth Power hails from diverse musical backgrounds, races and creeds, but the universal language of love and its power to change lives is the thread that binds them. The trio features drummer Nikki Glaspie, whose credentials include Beyonce, Maceo Parker, Dumpstaphunk and countless more; guitarist and lead vocalist Nick Cassarino, known best for his previous work with Jennifer Hartswick and Big Daddy Kane; and bassist Nate Edgar of reggae giant John Brown's Body.

item.title: Blue Nile presents Juice
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-05T18:00:00.000-06:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.bluenilelive.com/ Tickets: https://www.bluenilelive.com/
SINGLE RELEASE PARTY
for the NEW HIT “SUNDAY BEST”
Hailing from the 7th Ward neighborhood in New Orleans, Julian “Juice” Gosin is an artist, producer, trumpeter, MC, and educator. Best known for being a longtime member of award-winning and globally renowned brass band The Soul Rebels, Julian has worked with and been featured alongside Metallica, Nas, Lenny Kravitz, Wu-Tang Clan, Katy Perry, Maroon 5, and Maceo Parker. He has also recorded music with artists like Big Freedia, Trombone Shorty, G-Eazy, Phony ppl, and Dave East.
His recent debut album, Be Intentional, launched his solo career. The self-produced and written album has elements of hip hop, pop, RnB, and dance music and features Trombone Shorty, Nicolas Payton, and Nigel Hall.
All Shows Ages 21+

item.title: Blue Nile presents Kota Dosa
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-06T22:00:00.000-04:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.bluenilelive.com/
Kota Dosa is a creative music collective known for blending blues, funk, psychedelic rock, and jazz, with roots in New York, New Orleans, and Oregon. Led by brothers Jesse Jake and Cody Luke, the band features powerful vocals, guitar solos, and a notable horn section. They perform original music alongside influential covers and are recognized for their high-energy, genre-bending live shows.
AGES 21+. FREE ADMISSION WITH VALID I.D.

item.title: The magic is in the Gumbo. The bar will supply the booze, Flav and Kasia provide you 7 minutes to show us your stuff. Poets, Musicians, Comedians, Magicians and whatever you can think of within legal
item.description:
Venue: Poor Boys Starts: 2026-05-07T17:00:00.000-05:00 Categories: Comedy More info: https://www.facebook.com/share/14T5Mey6Nit/ Tickets: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/gumbolaya-the-magic-mic-tickets-1980178041828?aff=ebdsshcopyurl&utm-campaign=social&utm-content=attendeeshare&utm-medium=discovery&utm-term=listing&utm-source=cp
The magic is in the Gumbo. The bar will supply the booze, Flav and Kasia provide you 7 minutes to show us your stuff. Poets, Musicians, Comedians, Magicians and whatever you can think of within legal limits welcome to bring your flavor! Sometimes there's prizes, sometimes there's positive surprises in the mix. There's always fun! It's free, but buy 2 drinks dammit! Pull up, turn up, laugh and savor the flavor of the authentic New Orleans experience! Sign up at 9 start at 9:30. See less

item.title: Grammy Award-Winning Irvin Mayfield's Music Church
item.description:
Venue: Blue Nile Starts: 2026-05-07T21:00:00.000-05:00 Categories: Music - Cajun, Zydeco, Music - R&B, Funk, Brass Band, MG Indian, Music - Rock, Pop, Roots, Cover, Music - Classical, Music - Jazz & Blues, Music - Acoustic, Songwriter, Ethnic, Music - Other More info: https://www.showpass.com/irvin-mayfield-at-bluenile-2026/ Tickets: https://www.showpass.com/irvin-mayfield-at-bluenile-2026/
Irvin Mayfield is a Billboard and two-time Grammy Award- winning artist, composer, and producer. His 30+ record discography and published written works keynote three decades of world-tours, recorded performances, and collaborations with distinguished artists such as Stevie Wonder, Bob Weir, Frank Ocean, Aaron Neville, Wynton Marsalis, and Trombone Shorty.
A humanist born, raised, and presently residing in New Orleans, LA, Mayfield espouses a daily practice of constant reimagination in his community. His upcoming recording and live performance projects engage global thought leadership edging on the frontiers of art, media, technology and beauty.
9PM
Special Guests announced weekly.
Advance Tickets $20
Reserved Seating $25
VIP Area $400, Available as upgrade to your GA Ticket.
